sanskrit:
ये हि संस्पर्शजा भोगा दुःखयोनय एव ते।
आद्यन्तवन्तः कौन्तेय न तेषु रमते बुधः॥
transliteration:
ye hi saṁsparśajā bhogā duḥkha-yonaya eva te |
ādy-antavantaḥ kaunteya na teṣu ramate budhaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Those pleasures born of sense-contact are indeed wombs of sorrow; they have a beginning and an end, O Kaunteya—the wise do not delight in them.
contextual_meaning: Krishna warns that sensory highs are temporary distress disguised as happiness; the mature mind pivots toward lasting inner joy rather than chasing transient spikes.
psychological_state: Cognitive defusion from hedonic impulses; shift to eudaimonic well-being.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling late-night shopping apps, feeling the dopamine rush fade into buyer’s remorse.
anchor_text: True joy is within; chasing fleeting pleasures only breeds sorrow.
